hypothesis: Incorporating uncertainty-quantified concentration estimates from LC-MS as continuous probability distributions—rather than point values—as conditional inputs to the transformer decoder will disproportionately improve structure prediction accuracy for minority-concentration components (≤20%) in binary mixtures, because the model will learn to propagate concentration uncertainty into its attention weighting over spectral regions, thereby reducing the systematic bias toward dominant-component spectral features that currently causes performance degradation at low target-compound fractions.
